stories.headline: Getting by on a shoestring
stories.summary: Loraine Anderson discusses how the Traverse City Record-Eagle manages computer-assisted reporting with limited resources, sharing tips for small newspapers.

stories.headline: Joining Hands
stories.summary: Journalists at small newspapers are forming computer-assisted reporting networks to share training, data, and resources, with examples including CARwash in Washington state and similar consortiums in Wisconsin and across Knight Ridder publications.

stories.headline: Abatement analysis
stories.summary: A journalist's computer-assisted reporting project on tax abatements revealed flawed data, but the setback led to a comprehensive five-part series exposing how local municipalities were caught in bidding wars and how most relocated companies simply moved between nearby communities rather than bringing genuine economic growth.

stories.headline: Students rank schools
stories.summary: A University of Tennessee journalism class partnered with the LaFollette Press newspaper to produce an eight-page special report on Campbell County schools using state education database analysis, resulting in a comprehensive 'report card' of the school district's performance across multiple metrics.
